nodes.label: AI as collaborative conversational resource
nodes.grounding_span: being able to ask followup questions (for continuity) in having an actual conversation is nice

nodes.label: AI reliability and accuracy for medical information
nodes.grounding_span: Sometimes I need a quick answer and it needs to be reliable

nodes.label: AI as supplementary aide to human expertise
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for human expertise
nodes.grounding_span: I rely on my medical knowledge first and foremost, but having AI as a resource-rich tool is helpful

nodes.label: AI for repetitive and documentation tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI for sensitive and hands-on tasks
nodes.grounding_span: AI can be helpful at times with repetitive tasks such as charting and spelling

nodes.label: Efficient access to reliable information
nodes.grounding_span: The satisfaction comes with the ease of helpful information at my fingertips

nodes.label: Professional autonomy and human touch
nodes.grounding_span: I do not believe AI will ever be able to take over my work. There is too much human touch that is required

nodes.label: Workplace efficiency and reduced time
nodes.grounding_span: it has made things easier and less time-consuming

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: There has not been any real frustration with AI, as it has simply been a helpful tool

nodes.label: Confidence in irreplaceability of human skills
nodes.grounding_span: I do not believe AI will ever be able to take over my work

nodes.label: Conditional continued use based on helpfulness and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: As long as it is helpful and accurate, I will continue to use it

nodes.label: Iterative follow-up questioning for continuity
nodes.grounding_span: I will ask a question and then followup questions from there if necessary

nodes.label: Domain-based task delegation (sensitive vs. routine)
nodes.grounding_span: I handle all sensitive tasks myself, but I prefer to use AI for some general guidance
